title: Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant

seo.title: How to Make Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant — Easy Italian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant. This easy Italian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant
easy Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant recipe
authentic Italian Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant
lowCompetitionKeywords: Italian Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant Recipe
h2Headings: What is Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Italian Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant — a beloved Italian classic

description: Cornetto, the Italian cousin to the French croissant, offers a delightful start to any day with its tender, buttery layers and slightly sweeter dough. Perfect for a traditional Italian breakfast, these golden pastries are often enjoyed plain or filled with cream, jam, or chocolate. Master the art of making these flaky, aromatic treats right in your own cucina.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm milk, yeast, and a pinch of granulated sugar. Let it sit for 5 minutes until foamy. Add the remaining sugar, the large egg, all-purpose flour, and fine sea salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then knead on a lightly floured surface for about 3 minutes until smooth. Cover the dough and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare Butter Block
instructions.text: Take the cold unsalted butter and place it between two sheets of parchment paper. Using a rolling pin, roll the butter into a precise 15x15 cm (6x6 inch) square. Ensure the butter remains cold and solid. Place the butter block back in the refrigerator while you prepare the dough for the first turn.

instructions.title: First Lamination Fold
instructions.text: Roll the prepared dough into a 30x15 cm (12x6 inch) rectangle. Place the chilled butter block squarely on one half of the dough. Fold the other half of the dough over the butter, carefully sealing all the edges to fully enclose the butter. Gently roll the dough to slightly lengthen it, then fold it into thirds, like a letter. Cover and chill for 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Subsequent Lamination Folds
instructions.text: Repeat the rolling and folding process two more times. For each turn, roll the dough into a rectangle, fold it into thirds, cover, and chill for 5 minutes between each fold. This rapid lamination builds the layers quickly, crucial for a flaky texture. Work efficiently to keep the butter cold and prevent it from melting.

instructions.title: Shape the Cornetti
instructions.text: On a lightly floured surface, roll the laminated dough into a large, thin rectangle, about 3-4 mm thick. Trim the edges to create a neat rectangle. Cut the dough into long triangles with a base of about 8-10 cm (3-4 inches). Make a small slit at the center of the base of each triangle, then gently roll each triangle from the wide base towards the point to form crescent shapes.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Arrange the shaped cornetti on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. In a small bowl, whisk the egg yolk with 1 tablespoon of milk to create an egg wash. Brush the tops of the cornetti generously with the egg wash. Bake immediately in a preheated oven at 190C (375F) for 18-20 minutes, or until they are puffed, golden brown, and delightfully fragrant. Serve warm.

tips: The secret to perfectly flaky cornetti is keeping both the butter and the dough as cold as possible throughout the lamination process. Work quickly and use chilled tools when handling the dough.
When rolling the dough, apply even, gentle pressure to avoid tearing the layers of butter. This ensures a consistent rise and flakiness in the final product.

While this recipe offers a quicker active time, for a truly traditional cornetto, allow for a longer, cooler proofing (1-2 hours at room temperature or overnight in the fridge) to develop deeper flavor and a lighter, airier texture.
article: Cornetto Italian Butter Croissant is a beloved Italian Breakfast Recipes dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
